source_jobs.title: General Application
source_jobs.description:
Let’s stay in touch - or send great people our way!

Didn't find the right role this time? No problem! Leave us your contact details by clicking "Apply for Job", and we’ll reach out if something fitting comes up. Or do you know someone who’d be a great match for our team? Send them this way - we’d love to meet them!

source_jobs.title: Security Engineer - Blue Teamer
source_jobs.description:
At Trezor https://trezor.io/, security isn’t a checkbox. Security has always been at the core of what we do. From day one, we understood the risks of weak security practices. That’s why we didn’t just follow standards — we helped define them. We introduced the first hardware wallet and pioneered widely adopted security features such as Recovery seeds https://blog.trezor.io/seed-pin-passphrase-e15d14a0b546, Passphrases https://blog.trezor.io/seed-pin-passphrase-e15d14a0b546, and Shamir Backup https://trezor.io/learn/advanced/standards-proposals/what-is-shamir-backup — all of which contributed to our global success.

We’re now looking for a Security Engineer - Blue Teamer. You’ll join our growing security team where your main focus is going to be monitoring and implementation (as opposed to hacking) and internal security (as opposed to product security). Outside of the team, you can expect to cooperate mainly with our brilliant IT admins.




👉 WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Log management
   
   - You’ll take over our current system
   
   - Come up with your own ideas of what to monitor and implement them
   
   - Keep related documentation and processes updated
   
   - Respond to alerts and investigate

 - Implementation of security tools and integrations
   
   - You‘ll inherit our current tools for access management, alerting etc.
   
   - Come up with your own ideas of what to monitor and implement them

 - Security reviews
   
   - You’ll advise your colleagues on how to implement their ideas in a secure manner
   
   - Review vibe-coded internal apps from a security perspective

 - Endpoint security hardening
   
   - You’ll oversee MDM security settings, set up antivirus programs etc.
     
     


💪 ABOUT YOU

 - Minimum of 2 years in a similar, hands-on security role
   
   - Because you should be able to work independently and bring your own vision

 - Coding experience
   
   - Because you’ll write and review apps

 - Experience with some of the following technologies
   
   - Must have: Linux, Git, Github, Python
   
   - Nice to have: Slack apps, Graylog, Jenkins, Jamf Pro, Wazuh, Nessus, Suricata

 - Good communication skills
   
   - Because you will reach out to people to resolve incidents, you will consult implementations with IT as well as non-technical employees

 - Proficiency in English, Czech is a plus
   
   - Because English is the lingua franca of Trezor, while Czech is the mother tongue of most of our colleagues

 - On-site presence at least twice a week
   
   - Because it helps in relationship building and boosts efficiency
     
     


🤝 WHAT WE OFFER

 - A security-first company
   
   - Security is not just part of the role — it is part of how Trezor thinks, builds, and operates

 - Open-source mindset
   
   - You’ll join a company where transparency, trust, and security are not just values on paper, but part of the product and culture

 - Real ownership, no corporate layers
   
   - You’ll build internal applications, tools, and integrations that make people’s work easier, processes smoother, and the company more secure

 - Flexibility and support
   
   - Flexible working hours, a supportive team, and enough space to do your best work

 - Professional development
   
   - Budget for training programs, courses, conferences, and workshops of your choice

 - Friendly, open culture
   
   - Regular company events, informal get-togethers, and people who care about what they do

 - Option to get paid in bitcoin
   
   - Because we don’t just talk about Bitcoin — we use it

 - Perks and benefits
   
   - Renovated offices with a gym, massages, football table, billiards, PlayStation, 3D printer, free on-site parking, MultiSport card, company mobile tariff, yoga, fitness classes, and more
     
     

👋 Sounds good? Then we want to hear from you! Just submit your CV, together with a cover letter. We’ll get in touch with you as soon as we review your application, most likely within a week.

source_jobs.title: Electronics Engineer
source_jobs.description:
We're Trezor https://trezor.io/ - the inventors of the first hardware wallet and pioneers in crypto security. We've defined an entirely new product industry and continue to push the boundaries with cutting-edge innovations.

Our mission is to make self-custody accessible to everyone, and we're constantly striving to deliver the best possible trading experience to millions of users worldwide.

We are looking for Electronics Engineer, who will play a key role in shaping the hardware behind our innovative products. You’ll design and bring to life cutting-edge electronic solutions, working with modern technologies like wireless connectivity and power management. We’re looking for a skilled professional who’s passionate about electronics and ready to make a real impact.

Ready for the challenge? Let's dive into the details!



👉 What you'll do

 - Development of product electronics schematics, with a focus on products based on uPCs, wireless connectivity and wireless power management

 - PCB design

 - Components lifecycle maintenance based on product specifications and schematic design

 - Testing and measurement of prototypes

 - Creation of measurement protocols and technical documentation

 - Support with prototype validation, including EMC/RED certification

 - Collaboration with FW developers and test engineers

 - HW development of production tools, in cooperation with the testing team



💪 About you

 - Team player with a strong focus on delivering results and a proactive approach

 - Extensive experience with Altium Designer

 - Solid knowledge of digital and analog circuits

 - Knowledge of electronics measurements

 - Familiarity with EMC/RED standards is an advantage

 - Proficiency in Czech and English



🤝 What we offer

 - A unique opportunity to be part of a brand that has revolutionized the crypto industry more than once

 - Financial compensation based on your skills and experience, with the option to receive part of your compensation in Bitcoin

 - A budget for professional development (training programs, courses, and workshops of your choice)

 - A comfortable office space in Prague 8 (including a gym, massages, foosball, billiard, a snack-stocked kitchen, etc.), with free on-site parking

 - A friendly working environment, regular company events, and fun team get-togethers

 - Additional perks such as a MultiSport card or a company mobile plan



👋 Sounds good? We want to hear from you: just submit your CV along with a short cover letter. We'll definitely get in touch with you as soon as we review your application, most likely within a week.

source_jobs.title: Security Compliance Manager
source_jobs.description:
Tropic Square https://www.tropicsquare.com is building the world's first transparent, auditable security chip. Our engineering team knows how to build it. Now we need to make sure the company and the product meet the regulatory requirements that will define the market for the next decade.

TROPIC02, our upcoming production chip, must comply with the EU Cyber Resilience Act, pass EUCC certification under the Common Criteria, and hold up to external audit. We have engaged a specialist advisory firm to provide the regulatory and certification expertise. What we need internally is a Security Compliance Manager who makes the transformation actually happen — owns the programme, drives execution across the company, and ensures nothing falls through the cracks between our engineering, legal, management, and external consultants.

This is not a policy-writing role. It is a delivery role.




👉 WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own the compliance transformation programme. You’ll deliver Tropic Square's CRA compliance and EUCC certification programme end-to-end. You’ll build the master plan, set milestones, track progress, report to the CEO, resolve blockers, and re-plan when needed

 - Drive internal execution. Engineering teams documenting security architectures, process owners implementing SDL requirements, legal reviewing the Declaration of Conformity, operations setting up CSIRT procedures. You’ll coordinate them so everyone knows what is needed, by when, and why. You’ll own the internal action log and follow up relentlessly

 - Manage the advisory relationship. You’ll turn our external consultants’ recommendations on CRA, Common Criteria, IEC 62443, ISO 9001/27001/14001, and EUCC into clear internal tasks. You’ll ensure consultants get the inputs they need and challenge outputs that are missing, off-scope, or not fit for purpose

 - Coordinate across functions. Compliance transformation touches every part of the company: engineering, product, legal, finance, and leadership. You’ll run working groups, prepare CEO decision materials, align stakeholders on priorities, and keep everyone ahead of upcoming requirements, risks, and decisions

 - Track risks, costs, and decisions. You’ll maintain a live view of the programme's risk register, budget consumption, and open decisions. You’ll surface issues early and frame them clearly: what is the risk, what are the options, what is your recommendation. You do not wait to be asked
   
   


💪 WHO YOU ARE

You may come from programme management, consulting, operations, or a technical role where you ended up driving the project. What matters:

 - You get things done without formal authority. You’ve coordinated cross-functional programmes before, you can build trust quickly, keep busy stakeholders aligned, escalate without burning bridges, and make it easy for others to do what you need them to do

 - You can manage expert advisors without being one yourself. You know how to work with consultants: ask sharp questions, challenge outputs constructively, and turn their recommendations into internal action without losing anything in translation

 - You are structured and persistent. Complex programmes with many moving parts don't intimidate you — they motivate you. You maintain clarity on what needs to happen, who owns it, and by when. Details do not fall through the cracks

 - You communicate up and across with equal ease. You can prepare a clear and concise executive summary for the CEO and discuss technical topics with engineers in a way that builds trust. You understand that how you communicate is just as important as the message itself.

 - You learn fast. You don't need to be a certification expert and you don’t need to know AVA_VAN.3 on day one, but you can quickly understand CRA, Common Criteria, IEC 62443, and ISO standards well enough to manage the programme

What will help you ramp up faster:

 - Experience managing regulatory, certification, or compliance transformation programmes

 - Background in or e

source_jobs.title: Lead Software Engineer (Semiconductor Verification & Tooling)
source_jobs.description:
We're Tropic Square https://www.tropicsquare.com, a Prague-based company designing open-source secure-element chips.

We're looking for a Senior Software Engineer to join our hybrid team and help drive a long-term, open-source project at the intersection of software and hardware in the semiconductor world. You'll own core infrastructure decisions, shape architecture, and grow into a technical leadership role.

If you're excited by deep technical problems with real-world impact, this might be the role for you.




👉 WHAT YOU'LL WORK ON

 - Developing and expanding our automated flows and hardware modelling framework

 - Building internal tooling and infrastructure that serves both the company and the wider open-source community

 - Driving architectural decisions and the long-term technical direction of the codebase

 - Exploring adjacent technical areas (verification, cybersecurity, EDA) as the work demands, with room to go deep where you're curious

 - Collaborating with internal teams and external contributors in a public, technically demanding setting
   
   


✍️ OUR STACK

 - Mostly Python, Bash, C, and whatever else the job calls for

 - Check out our Github https://github.com/tropicsquare/ts-tvl
   
   


💪 WHO YOU ARE

 - Senior software engineer with a track record of designing and maintaining complex systems

 - Either you've led a small team and want to keep doing it, or you're a senior IC with the instincts to mentor and steer without formal reports

 - Background or genuine interest in hardware, EDA, or embedded development

 - Experience or interest in security. We build in a domain where it's not optional

 - Comfortable thinking in terms of trade-offs and second-order effects, not just shipping the next feature

 - At home in a Unix/Linux environment and in open-source workflows (public reviews, issue trackers, async collaboration)
   
   


🤝 WHY JOIN US

 - A rare role where software architecture and hardware reality meet

 - Direct, visible impact on an open-source project used in real semiconductor development

 - Hybrid by default: work from the Prague office, remotely, or mix the two, whichever fits your life. We optimize for autonomy and deep focus

 - In-person team meetups in Prague are held a few times a year for planning and architecture work
   
   

👋 Interested? We want to hear from you! Just submit your CV, a brief cover letter, and links to your work. After the initial screening, you'll be directly in touch with your future team.

notes: Multiple engineering roles, no explicit sponsorship or clearance info
